{"society_power": 6.5, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Pullman Strike crushed; state coercion and Spanish-American War expansion", "reasoning": "Despite intense populist and labor mobilization, defeats at Homestead, Pullman (federal troops, In re Debs), the 1896 Bryan loss, Jim Crow entrenchment (Plessy), and trust consolidation left society weaker on balance; meanwhile the Sherman Act, court-backed coercion, and the Spanish-American War expanded federal administrative and military capacity."}
